package handler

import (
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
	"net/http"
)

// Positive scenario for gin_handler_runs_authorization_business_rules_inline
// Rule intent: Handlers that contain complex permission matrices and business logic
// instead of delegating to policy or service layers.
func PositiveGinHandlerRunsAuthorizationBusinessRulesInline(c *gin.Context, db *gorm.DB) {
	role := c.GetHeader("X-Role")
	userID := c.Param("id")
	tenantID := c.GetHeader("X-Tenant-ID")

	if role != "admin" {
		c.JSON(http.StatusForbidden, gin.H{"error": "forbidden"})
		return
	}

	var user struct {
		ID       uint   `gorm:"primaryKey"`
		Role     string `gorm:"column:role"`
		TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id"`
		Status   string `gorm:"column:status"`
		Balance  int    `gorm:"column:balance"`
	}
	db.Where("id = ?", userID).First(&user)

	if user.TenantID != tenantID {
		c.JSON(http.StatusForbidden, gin.H{"error": "tenant mismatch"})
		return
	}

	if user.Status == "suspended" {
		c.JSON(http.StatusForbidden, gin.H{"error": "account suspended"})
		return
	}

	if user.Balance < 0 && role != "admin" {
		c.JSON(http.StatusPaymentRequired, gin.H{"error": "insufficient balance"})
		return
	}

	if user.Role == "manager" && user.Balance > 10000 {
		c.JSON(http.StatusForbidden, gin.H{"error": "manager limit exceeded"})
		return
	}

	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"user": user, "authorized": true})
}
